news package¶

Submodules¶

news.admin module¶

class news.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for Article, with a bulk-approve action.

actions = ['approve_selected']¶
approve_selected(request, queryset)[source]¶

Approve all selected unapproved articles via the admin action.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'submitted', 'approved', 'created_at')¶
list_filter = ('approved', 'submitted', 'publisher')¶
property media¶
search_fields = ('title', 'author__username')¶
class news.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for Newsletter.

filter_horizontal = ('articles',)¶
list_display = ('title', 'author', 'created_at')¶
property media¶
class news.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for Publisher.

list_display = ('name', 'website', 'created_at')¶
property media¶
search_fields = ('name',)¶

news.api_urls module¶

API URL configuration for the news app.

Registered under /api/ in newsapp/urls.py. JWT token endpoints are included here so all API routes share the /api/ prefix.

news.api_views module¶

news.api_views¶

Django REST Framework API views for the NewsApp news module.

Endpoints¶

Publishers

GET/POST /api/publishers/ — list or create publishers. GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /api/publishers/<pk>/ — detail, update, delete.

Articles

GET/POST /api/articles/ — list (role-filtered) or create. GET /api/articles/subscribed/ — reader’s subscription feed. GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /api/articles/<pk>/ — detail, update, delete. PATCH /api/articles/<pk>/approve/ — editor approval.

Newsletters

GET/POST /api/newsletters/ — list or create. GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /api/newsletters/<pk>/ — detail, update, delete.

JWT Auth

POST /api/token/ — obtain access + refresh tokens. POST /api/token/refresh/ — refresh access token.

class news.api_views.ArticleApproveView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: APIView

Approve a submitted article.

PATCH /api/articles/<pk>/approve/ — editors only. Sets approved=True and fires the _just_approved signal so notification emails are sent to subscribed readers.

Permission_classes:

IsEditorOrReadOnly

patch(request, pk)[source]¶

Approve the article identified by pk.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the article to approve.

Returns:

Serialized article data on success, or a 400 error if the article is already approved.

Return type:

Response

permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.IsEditorOrReadOnly'>]¶
class news.api_views.ArticleDetailView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

Retrieve, update, or delete a single article.

  • GET — unapproved articles visible to their author or editors.

  • PUT / PATCH — author or editor only.

  • DELETE — author or editor only.

Permission_classes:

IsApprovedOrAuthorOrEditor, IsAuthorOrEditor

permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.IsApprovedOrAuthorOrEditor'>, <class 'news.permissions.IsAuthorOrEditor'>]¶
queryset = <QuerySet [<Article: Article more – Journalist1>, <Article: The Writing – Journalist1>]>¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.api_views.ArticleListCreateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

List articles (role-filtered) or create a new article.

  • GET — approved articles for anonymous/readers; editors see all; journalists see approved plus their own drafts.

  • POST — journalists only; author is set from the request.

Permission_classes:

IsJournalistOrReadOnly

get_queryset()[source]¶

Return articles visible to the requesting user.

Returns:

Queryset of articles filtered by the user’s role.

Return type:

QuerySet

perform_create(serializer)[source]¶

Set the author to the logged-in journalist before saving.

Parameters:

serializer (ArticleSerializer) – The validated article serializer instance.

permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.IsJournalistOrReadOnly'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.api_views.ArticleSubscribedListView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListAPIView

Return approved articles from a reader’s subscriptions.

GET /api/articles/subscribed/ — authenticated readers only. Returns articles from the reader’s subscribed publishers and journalists. Non-readers receive an empty list.

Permission_classes:

IsAuthenticated

get_queryset()[source]¶

Return articles the requesting reader subscribes to.

Returns:

Queryset of approved articles from subscribed sources, or an empty queryset for non-readers.

Return type:

QuerySet

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class news.api_views.NewsletterDetailView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

Retrieve, update, or delete a single newsletter.

  • GET — public; returns newsletter detail with articles.

  • PUT / PATCH / DELETE — author or editor only.

Permission_classes:

IsAuthorOrEditor

permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.IsAuthorOrEditor'>]¶
queryset = <QuerySet [<Newsletter: The events by Journalist1>]>¶
serializer_class¶

alias of NewsletterSerializer

class news.api_views.NewsletterListCreateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

List all newsletters or create a new one.

  • GET — public; returns all newsletters ordered by newest first.

  • POST — journalists only; author is set from the request.

Permission_classes:

IsJournalistOrReadOnly

perform_create(serializer)[source]¶

Set the author to the logged-in journalist before saving.

Parameters:

serializer (NewsletterSerializer) – The validated newsletter serializer instance.

permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.IsJournalistOrReadOnly'>]¶
queryset = <QuerySet [<Newsletter: The events by Journalist1>]>¶
serializer_class¶

alias of NewsletterSerializer

class news.api_views.PublisherDetailView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

Retrieve, update, or delete a single publisher.

  • GET — public; returns publisher detail.

  • PUT / PATCH / DELETE — editors only.

Permission_classes:

IsEditorOrReadOnly

permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.IsEditorOrReadOnly'>]¶
queryset = <QuerySet [<Publisher: BIGPublish>]>¶
serializer_class¶

alias of PublisherSerializer

class news.api_views.PublisherListCreateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

List all publishers or create a new one.

  • GET — public; returns all publishers ordered by name.

  • POST — editors only; creates a new publisher.

Permission_classes:

IsEditorOrReadOnly

permission_classes = [<class 'news.permissions.IsEditorOrReadOnly'>]¶
queryset = <QuerySet [<Publisher: BIGPublish>]>¶
serializer_class¶

alias of PublisherSerializer

news.apps module¶

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]¶

Bases: AppConfig

App configuration for the news application.

name = 'news'¶
ready()[source]¶

Connect signal handlers when the app registry is fully populated.

news.decorators module¶

news.decorators¶

Role-based access-control decorators for NewsApp views.

Decorators¶

journalist_required

Restrict a view to authenticated users with role='journalist'.

editor_required

Restrict a view to authenticated users with role='editor'.

reader_required

Restrict a view to authenticated users with role='reader'.

login_required_custom

Require authentication only; object-level role checks are handled inside the view itself.

news.decorators.editor_required(view_func)[source]¶

Restrict the decorated view to users with the Editor role.

Anonymous users are redirected to the login page. Authenticated users with a different role receive a 403 response.

Parameters:

view_func (callable) – The view function to wrap.

Returns:

The wrapped view enforcing editor-only access.

Return type:

callable

news.decorators.journalist_required(view_func)[source]¶

Restrict the decorated view to users with the Journalist role.

Anonymous users are redirected to the login page. Authenticated users with a different role receive a 403 response.

Parameters:

view_func (callable) – The view function to wrap.

Returns:

The wrapped view enforcing journalist-only access.

Return type:

callable

news.decorators.login_required_custom(view_func)[source]¶

Require the user to be authenticated.

Used for views that perform their own object-level role checks internally (e.g. edit/delete where the article author and editors are both permitted). Anonymous users are redirected to the login page.

Parameters:

view_func (callable) – The view function to wrap.

Returns:

The wrapped view enforcing authentication.

Return type:

callable

news.decorators.reader_required(view_func)[source]¶

Restrict the decorated view to users with the Reader role.

Anonymous users are redirected to the login page. Authenticated users with a different role receive a 403 response.

Parameters:

view_func (callable) – The view function to wrap.

Returns:

The wrapped view enforcing reader-only access.

Return type:

callable

news.forms module¶

news.forms¶

Django ModelForms for the NewsApp news module.

Forms¶

ArticleForm

Create and edit articles; author is set by the view.

NewsletterForm

Create and edit newsletters; filters articles to approved only.

PublisherForm

Create and edit publishers; restricted to editors.

class news.forms.ArticleForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: BootstrapFormMixin, ModelForm

Form for creating and editing an Article.

The publisher field is optional (independent articles have no publisher). The image field is optional. The author field is excluded and set by the view from the logged-in user.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Form configuration: model, fields, and widget overrides.

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher', 'image']¶
model¶

alias of Article

widgets = {'content': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>}¶
base_fields = {'content': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'image': <django.forms.fields.ImageField object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.models.ModelChoiceField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.NewsletterForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: BootstrapFormMixin, ModelForm

Form for creating and editing a Newsletter.

The articles M2M field is rendered as checkboxes so the journalist can pick which articles to include. Only approved articles are selectable so that unpublished drafts cannot appear in a newsletter. The author field is excluded and set by the view.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Form configuration: model, fields, and widget overrides.

fields = ['title', 'description', 'articles']¶
model¶

alias of Newsletter

widgets = {'articles': <django.forms.widgets.CheckboxSelectMultiple object>, 'description': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>}¶
base_fields = {'articles': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>, 'description': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.PublisherForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: BootstrapFormMixin, ModelForm

Form for creating and editing a Publisher.

Only editors can access this form. The logo and website fields are optional.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Form configuration: model, fields, and widget overrides.

fields = ['name', 'description', 'logo', 'website']¶
model¶

alias of Publisher

widgets = {'description': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>}¶
base_fields = {'description': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'logo': <django.forms.fields.ImageField object>, 'name': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'website': <django.forms.fields.URLField object>}¶
declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

news.models module¶

news.models¶

Database models for the NewsApp news module.

Models¶

Publisher

A named publication that groups journalists and articles.

Article

A news article written by a journalist, subject to editor approval.

Newsletter

A curated collection of articles created by a journalist.

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

A news article authored by a Journalist.

An article is associated with EITHER a publisher (publisher-content) OR no publisher (independent). It is visible to readers only once an Editor sets approved=True.

Variables:

  • title (str) – Headline of the article (max 300 characters).

  • content (str) – Full body text of the article.

  • author (CustomUser) – The journalist who wrote the article.

  • publisher (Publisher or None) – The publisher this article belongs to, or None for independent articles.

  • created_at (datetime) – Timestamp set automatically when the record is created.

  • updated_at (datetime) – Timestamp updated automatically on every save.

  • approved (bool) – True once an editor has approved the article for public viewing.

  • submitted (bool) – True when the journalist has submitted the article for editorial review.

  • image (ImageField) – Optional article image stored under article_images/.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶
content¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_next_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

image¶

Just like the FileDescriptor, but for ImageFields. The only difference is assigning the width/height to the width_field/height_field, if appropriate.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
publisher¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id¶
submitted¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

title¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

updated_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

A curated collection of articles created by a Journalist.

Readers can view newsletters; Journalists and Editors can create and edit them. The many-to-many relation to Article allows the same article to appear in multiple newsletters.

Variables:

  • title (str) – Headline of the newsletter (max 300 characters).

  • description (str) – Optional summary of the newsletter’s contents.

  • author (CustomUser) – The user who created the newsletter.

  • articles (ManyToManyField(Article)) – The articles included in this newsletter.

  • created_at (datetime) – Timestamp set automatically when the record is created.

  • updated_at (datetime) – Timestamp updated automatically on every save.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶
created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

description¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_next_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
title¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

updated_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

A named publication affiliated with multiple journalists and editors.

Journalists link via the reverse relation on CustomUser’s subscribed_publishers M2M field, keeping the schema normalised. A Publisher is the optional parent of an Article.

Variables:

  • name (str) – Unique display name of the publisher.

  • description (str) – Optional long-form description of the publication.

  • logo (ImageField) – Optional logo image stored under publisher_logos/.

  • website (str) – Optional URL of the publisher’s external website.

  • created_at (datetime) – Timestamp set automatically when the record is created.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

description¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

logo¶

Just like the FileDescriptor, but for ImageFields. The only difference is assigning the width/height to the width_field/height_field, if appropriate.

name¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
subscribers¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

website¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

news.permissions module¶

news.permissions¶

Custom Django REST Framework permission classes for the NewsApp API.

Each class encapsulates a single access-control rule so API views remain readable and focused on business logic.

Permissions¶

IsEditorOrReadOnly

Read access for everyone; write access for editors only.

IsJournalistOrReadOnly

Read access for everyone; write access for journalists only.

IsAuthorOrEditor

Object-level: allow modification by the object’s author or editors.

IsApprovedOrAuthorOrEditor

Object-level: unapproved articles visible only to author or editors.

class news.permissions.IsApprovedOrAuthorOrEditor[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Object-level read permission for articles.

Approved articles are visible to everyone. Unapproved articles are visible only to their author or any editor.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]¶

Return True if the user may view this article.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • view (APIView) – The view being accessed.

  • obj – The Article instance being accessed.

Returns:

True if approved, or user is the author/an editor.

Return type:

bool

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Always return True; visibility is enforced at object level.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • view (APIView) – The view being accessed.

Returns:

Always True.

Return type:

bool

class news.permissions.IsAuthorOrEditor[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Object-level permission allowing modification by author or editors.

Safe HTTP methods are always permitted. Write methods require the requesting user to be either the object’s author or an editor.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]¶

Return True if the user may act on this specific object.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • view (APIView) – The view being accessed.

  • obj – The model instance being accessed.

Returns:

True for safe methods, editors, or the author.

Return type:

bool

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True if the request passes view-level checks.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • view (APIView) – The view being accessed.

Returns:

True for safe methods or authenticated users.

Return type:

bool

class news.permissions.IsEditorOrReadOnly[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow read access to everyone; write access to editors only.

Safe HTTP methods (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS) are permitted for all users including anonymous. All other methods require the requesting user to be authenticated with the editor role.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True if the request should be permitted.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • view (APIView) – The view being accessed.

Returns:

True for safe methods or authenticated editors.

Return type:

bool

class news.permissions.IsJournalistOrReadOnly[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow read access to everyone; write access to journalists only.

Safe HTTP methods (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS) are permitted for all users including anonymous. All other methods require the requesting user to be authenticated with the journalist role.

has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True if the request should be permitted.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • view (APIView) – The view being accessed.

Returns:

True for safe methods or authenticated journalists.

Return type:

bool

news.serializers module¶

news.serializers¶

Django REST Framework serializers for the NewsApp news module.

Each serializer maps one model to its JSON representation used by the API. Read (list/detail) and write (create/update) operations are handled by the same class where possible. The author field is always set from the request context in the view, never accepted from the client.

Serializers¶

PublisherSerializer

Full read/write serializer for Publisher.

ArticleSerializer

Serializer for Article, with a read-only author_username field.

NewsletterSerializer

Serializer for Newsletter, exposing articles as a list of PKs.

class news.serializers.ArticleApprovalSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Minimal serializer used by the editor approval endpoint.

Only exposes the approved flag so that clients cannot accidentally overwrite other article fields via this endpoint.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Serializer configuration.

fields = ['id', 'approved']¶
model¶

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ['id']¶
class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for the Article model.

author_username is a read-only convenience field so API consumers can display the author’s name without a separate request. author (PK) is set automatically by the view and excluded from writeable input.

Variables:

  • id (int) – Auto-generated primary key (read-only).

  • title (str) – Headline of the article.

  • content (str) – Full body text of the article.

  • author (int) – PK of the authoring journalist (read-only).

  • author_username (str) – Username of the author (read-only).

  • publisher (int or None) – PK of the associated publisher, or null.

  • submitted (bool) – Whether the article has been submitted for review.

  • approved (bool) – Whether the article has been approved (read-only).

  • image (str) – URL path to the article image, if any.

  • created_at (str) – ISO-8601 creation timestamp (read-only).

  • updated_at (str) – ISO-8601 last-updated timestamp (read-only).

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Serializer configuration.

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'author_username', 'publisher', 'submitted', 'approved', 'image', 'created_at', 'updated_at']¶
model¶

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ['id', 'author', 'author_username', 'approved', 'created_at', 'updated_at']¶
class news.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for the Newsletter model.

article_ids exposes the M2M relation as a list of primary keys, restricted to approved articles only. author_username is a read-only convenience field.

Variables:

  • id (int) – Auto-generated primary key (read-only).

  • title (str) – Headline of the newsletter.

  • description (str) – Optional summary of the newsletter’s contents.

  • author (int) – PK of the authoring journalist (read-only).

  • author_username (str) – Username of the author (read-only).

  • article_ids (list[int]) – List of PKs of approved articles to include.

  • created_at (str) – ISO-8601 creation timestamp (read-only).

  • updated_at (str) – ISO-8601 last-updated timestamp (read-only).

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Serializer configuration.

fields = ['id', 'title', 'description', 'author', 'author_username', 'article_ids', 'created_at', 'updated_at']¶
model¶

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ['id', 'author', 'author_username', 'created_at', 'updated_at']¶
class news.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for the Publisher model.

Exposes all public-facing fields. Creation and updates are restricted to editors via the API view’s permission class.

Variables:

  • id (int) – Auto-generated primary key (read-only).

  • name (str) – Unique display name of the publisher.

  • description (str) – Optional long-form description.

  • logo (str) – URL path to the publisher’s logo image.

  • website (str) – Optional external website URL.

  • created_at (str) – ISO-8601 creation timestamp (read-only).

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Serializer configuration.

fields = ['id', 'name', 'description', 'logo', 'website', 'created_at']¶
model¶

alias of Publisher

read_only_fields = ['id', 'created_at']¶
class news.serializers.UserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Read-only serializer for the CustomUser model.

Exposes safe public-facing fields only — the password hash is never included.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Serializer configuration.

fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'role', 'bio']¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'role']¶

news.signals module¶

Signal handlers for the news app.

Listens for the post_save signal on Article and fires notification logic when an article is approved for the first time.

news.signals.on_article_approved(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]¶

Fire notifications when an article is approved for the first time.

The _just_approved flag is set by the approve_article view and the approve_selected admin action immediately before article.save(). Without that flag this handler is a no-op, which prevents spurious emails on every subsequent edit.

news.tests module¶

Unit tests for the news app.

Covers models, web views (access control + happy paths), signals, and REST API endpoints.

class news.tests.ArticleAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the /api/articles/ endpoints.

setUp()[source]¶

Create users and an approved article; set up APIClient.

test_journalist_can_create_article()[source]¶

Authenticated journalist can POST a new article.

test_list_returns_approved_articles()[source]¶

GET /api/articles/ returns approved articles to anonymous users.

test_reader_cannot_create_article()[source]¶

A reader POSTing to /api/articles/ gets 403.

test_unauthenticated_cannot_create_article()[source]¶

Unauthenticated POST to /api/articles/ returns 401 or 403.

class news.tests.ArticleApprovalTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the editor approval workflow and signal side-effects.

setUp()[source]¶

Create an editor and a pending article.

test_approve_sends_email_to_subscribers(mock_tweet, mock_mail)[source]¶

Approving an article triggers send_mail for subscribed readers.

test_approve_sets_approved_flag(mock_tweet, mock_mail)[source]¶

POSTing to approve_article sets article.approved = True.

class news.tests.ArticleApproveAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for PATCH /api/articles/<pk>/approve/.

setUp()[source]¶

Create editor, journalist, and a pending article.

test_editor_can_approve_via_api(mock_tweet, mock_mail)[source]¶

An editor PATCHing the approve endpoint sets approved=True.

test_non_editor_cannot_approve_via_api()[source]¶

A journalist PATCHing the approve endpoint gets 403.

class news.tests.ArticleDeleteAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for DELETE /api/articles/<pk>/.

setUp()[source]¶

Create editor, journalist, and a published article.

test_editor_can_delete_article()[source]¶

An editor can DELETE an article.

test_reader_cannot_delete_article()[source]¶

A reader cannot DELETE an article.

class news.tests.ArticleListViewTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the public article list view.

setUp()[source]¶

Create one approved and one unapproved article.

test_article_list_returns_200()[source]¶

Public home page returns HTTP 200.

test_only_approved_articles_shown()[source]¶

Unapproved articles are not shown on the public list.

class news.tests.ArticleModelTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the Article model.

setUp()[source]¶

Create a journalist for use in article tests.

test_approved_defaults_to_false()[source]¶

New articles are unapproved by default.

test_str_includes_title_and_author()[source]¶

__str__ contains both the title and the author’s username.

class news.tests.ArticleSubscribedAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for GET /api/articles/subscribed/.

setUp()[source]¶

Create users, articles, and subscriptions.

test_reader_gets_subscribed_articles()[source]¶

Authenticated reader sees only their subscribed content.

test_unauthenticated_cannot_access_subscribed()[source]¶

Unauthenticated GET /api/articles/subscribed/ is rejected.

class news.tests.DecoratorAccessTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests that role decorators block wrong roles with 403.

setUp()[source]¶

Create one user of each role.

test_editor_required_allows_editor()[source]¶

An editor can access the review queue.

test_editor_required_blocks_journalist()[source]¶

A journalist hitting an editor-only URL gets 403.

test_journalist_required_allows_journalist()[source]¶

A journalist can access the create-article page.

test_journalist_required_blocks_reader()[source]¶

A reader hitting a journalist-only URL gets 403.

test_unauthenticated_user_redirected_to_login()[source]¶

An anonymous user is redirected to the login page.

class news.tests.NewsletterAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the /api/newsletters/ endpoints.

setUp()[source]¶

Create journalist, reader, article, and newsletter.

test_journalist_can_create_newsletter()[source]¶

Authenticated journalist can POST a new newsletter.

test_list_newsletters_is_public()[source]¶

GET /api/newsletters/ returns 200 for anonymous users.

test_reader_cannot_create_newsletter()[source]¶

A reader POSTing to /api/newsletters/ gets 403.

class news.tests.NewsletterModelTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the Newsletter model.

setUp()[source]¶

Create a journalist for newsletter tests.

test_str_includes_title_and_author()[source]¶

__str__ contains both the title and the author’s username.

class news.tests.PublisherAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the /api/publishers/ endpoints.

setUp()[source]¶

Create users and a publisher.

test_editor_can_create_publisher()[source]¶

An editor can POST a new publisher.

test_journalist_cannot_create_publisher()[source]¶

A journalist POSTing to /api/publishers/ gets 403.

test_list_publishers_is_public()[source]¶

GET /api/publishers/ returns 200 for anonymous users.

class news.tests.PublisherModelTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the Publisher model.

test_str_returns_name()[source]¶

__str__ returns the publisher name.

news.tests.make_article(author, publisher=None, approved=False, title='Test Article')[source]¶

Create and return an Article.

news.tests.make_newsletter(author, title='Test Newsletter')[source]¶

Create and return a Newsletter.

news.tests.make_publisher(name='Test Publisher')[source]¶

Create and return a Publisher.

news.tests.make_user(username, role, password='Pass1234!')[source]¶

Create and return a CustomUser with the given role.

news.twitter module¶

Twitter / X integration for the news app.

Singleton service that wraps requests-oauthlib to post tweets (with optional image attachment) via the X v2 API. Credentials are read from Django settings, which in turn reads them from .env via python-decouple — nothing sensitive is hard-coded.

class news.twitter.TwitterService[source]¶

Bases: object

Singleton service for posting to X (Twitter) via API v2.

tweet(text, image_field=None)[source]¶

Post a tweet with optional image.

Returns True on success, False on failure.

upload_media(image_field)[source]¶

Upload an image to X. Returns the media_id string, or None.

news.urls module¶

URL configuration for the news app web views.

API endpoints are in news/api_urls.py and registered under /api/ in newsapp/urls.py.

news.utils module¶

Utility functions for the news app.

send_approval_emails — notify subscribers when an article is approved. tweet_approved_article — post a tweet via TwitterService (best-effort).

news.utils.send_approval_emails(article)[source]¶

Send a notification email to every subscriber who follows the article’s author or publisher.

Deduplication is handled by collecting recipient addresses into a set before sending so that readers who follow both the journalist and the publisher only receive one email.

news.utils.tweet_approved_article(article)[source]¶

Post a tweet announcing the newly approved article.

Attaches the article image if one exists. Failures are swallowed silently so that a missing or invalid Twitter configuration never breaks the approval workflow.

news.views module¶

news.views¶

HTTP view functions for the NewsApp news module.

View groups¶

Article views

List, detail, create, edit, delete, and editor approval queue.

Newsletter views

List, detail, create, edit, and delete newsletters.

Publisher views

Create, edit, delete, and detail pages for publishers.

Journalist profile

Public profile page showing a journalist’s approved articles.

Subscription views

Reader toggle-subscription endpoints for publishers and journalists.

news.views.approve_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Approve a pending article.

Sets the private _just_approved flag on the instance before saving so the post_save signal can distinguish a new approval from an ordinary re-save, avoiding duplicate notification emails or tweets.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the article to approve.

Returns:

Redirect to the editor queue.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

news.views.article_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

Display a single article.

Unapproved articles are visible only to the article’s author and editors. All other users receive a 403 response for unapproved articles.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the article to display.

Returns:

Rendered article detail page, or a 403 response.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.article_list(request)[source]¶

Display a paginated list of all approved articles, newest first.

Accessible to all users (authenticated and anonymous). Results are paginated at 9 articles per page.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered article list page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.create_article(request)[source]¶

Allow a journalist to draft or submit an article for review.

Two submit buttons drive the behaviour:

  • Save Draft — saves with submitted=False.

  • Submit for Review — saves with submitted=True, queuing for editor review.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered article form on GET/invalid POST, or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.create_newsletter(request)[source]¶

Allow a journalist to create a new newsletter.

The logged-in user is automatically set as the author before saving.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered newsletter form on GET/invalid POST, or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.create_publisher(request)[source]¶

Allow an editor to create a new publisher from the web interface.

On GET renders a blank PublisherForm. On POST validates and saves; redirects to the new publisher’s detail page.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered publisher form on GET/invalid POST, or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.delete_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow the article’s author or any editor to delete an article.

Displays a confirmation page on GET and performs the deletion on POST.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the article to delete.

Returns:

Rendered confirmation page on GET, or redirect on POST.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.delete_newsletter(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow the newsletter’s author or any editor to delete a newsletter.

Displays a confirmation page on GET and performs the deletion on POST.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the newsletter to delete.

Returns:

Rendered confirmation page on GET, or redirect on POST.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.delete_publisher(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow an editor to delete a publisher.

Displays a confirmation page on GET and performs the deletion on POST. After deletion, redirects to the article list.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the publisher to delete.

Returns:

Rendered confirmation page on GET, or redirect on POST.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.edit_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow the article’s author or any editor to edit an article.

A journalist who did not author the article receives a 403 response.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the article to edit.

Returns:

Rendered article form on GET/invalid POST, or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.edit_newsletter(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow the newsletter’s author or any editor to edit a newsletter.

A journalist who did not author the newsletter receives a 403 response.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the newsletter to edit.

Returns:

Rendered newsletter form on GET/invalid POST, or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.edit_publisher(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow an editor to update an existing publisher’s details.

On GET renders a pre-filled PublisherForm. On POST validates and saves; redirects to the publisher’s detail page.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the publisher to edit.

Returns:

Rendered publisher form on GET/invalid POST, or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.editor_queue(request)[source]¶

Display all unapproved articles that have been submitted for review.

Articles are ordered by submission date (oldest first) so editors process them in the order they were received. Accessible only to editors.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered editor queue page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]¶

Show the logged-in journalist their own articles and newsletters.

Articles are ordered newest-first so the journalist can see recent work at a glance, including which drafts are pending review.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request (must be an authenticated journalist).

Returns:

Rendered journalist dashboard page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.journalist_profile(request, pk)[source]¶

Display a journalist’s profile page and their approved articles.

Readers see a Subscribe / Unsubscribe toggle button based on whether they are already subscribed to this journalist.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the journalist (CustomUser) to display.

Returns:

Rendered journalist profile page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.newsletter_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

Display a single newsletter with its included articles.

Accessible to all users (authenticated and anonymous).

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the newsletter to display.

Returns:

Rendered newsletter detail page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.newsletter_list(request)[source]¶

Display a list of all newsletters, newest first.

Accessible to all users (authenticated and anonymous).

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered newsletter list page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.publisher_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

Display a publisher’s profile page and their latest approved articles.

Readers see a Subscribe / Unsubscribe toggle button based on whether they are already subscribed to this publisher.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the publisher to display.

Returns:

Rendered publisher detail page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.reject_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Return a pending article to the journalist without approving it.

The article remains in the queue (approved=False); a flash message notifies the editor that the article was returned.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the article to reject.

Returns:

Redirect to the editor queue.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

news.views.subscribe_journalist(request, pk)[source]¶

Toggle the current reader’s subscription to a journalist.

On POST, adds or removes the journalist from the reader’s subscribed_journalists M2M field and shows a flash message. Redirects back to the journalist’s profile page.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request (must be an authenticated reader).

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the journalist (CustomUser) to subscribe/unsubscribe from.

Returns:

Redirect to the journalist profile page.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

news.views.subscribe_publisher(request, pk)[source]¶

Toggle the current reader’s subscription to a publisher.

On POST, adds or removes the publisher from the reader’s subscribed_publishers M2M field and shows a flash message. Redirects back to the publisher’s profile page.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request (must be an authenticated reader).

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the publisher to subscribe/unsubscribe from.

Returns:

Redirect to the publisher detail page.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect
